diagrams.title: Null Pointer Waste in Binary Tree
diagrams.description: Shows n+1 null pointers in a sample binary tree that can be converted to threads
elements: Left leaf node with 2 null pointers (marked as waste)
Right leaf node with 2 null pointers (marked as waste)
Total null pointers shown in red circles
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the problem that threaded trees solve - wasted null pointer space

diagrams.title: Right-Inorder Threaded Tree
diagrams.description: Converts a binary tree to right-inorder threaded form with threads shown as dashed arrows
elements: Right threads pointing to inorder successor (dashed arrows)
Tag values shown at each node (ltag, rtag)
0 values indicate thread, 1 values indicate actual child
diagrams.purpose: Understand how threads are created and tag values indicate thread vs child

diagrams.title: Inorder Traversal in Threaded Tree
diagrams.description: Step-by-step traversal showing the path through a threaded tree
elements: Start at leftmost node (first to visit)
Arrow showing movement via right threads
Arrows showing when to descend to right child (rtag=1)
Sequence numbers showing order of node visits
Visual distinction between thread edges and tree edges
diagrams.purpose: Learn the traversal algorithm visually by following the arrows

diagrams.title: Single vs Double Threading Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of single-threaded and double-threaded node structures
elements: Single-threaded node: 4 fields (data, left, right, rtag)
Double-threaded node: 5 fields (data, left, right, ltag, rtag)
Arrows showing thread directions for both types
Example tree converted to both formats
diagrams.purpose: Understand structural differences and storage requirements
